depends_on.context: External cross-skill dependency (ADR-005): the shared MCP execution substrate all six modes reach through the unchanged code_mode MCP registration key — the three workflow bridges (mcp-chrome-devtools, mcp-click-up, mcp-aside-devtools) as their MCP fallback lane, and the three transports (mcp-figma via the optional Framelink manual, mcp-refero and mcp-mobbin as remote MCP through Code Mode). mcp-code-mode is infrastructure serving consumers beyond this hub, not a hub member.

The mandatory cross-hub judgment pairing (ADR-002) for all three design transports: apply sk-design's anti-default judgment before any design-affecting operation — mcp-figma reads/exports feeding a design decision AND its author/modify/token paths, mcp-refero and mcp-mobbin whenever their references ground a design decision. Also a sibling overlap -- sk-design's own md-generator mode sources design tokens from live URLs while mcp-figma sources from Figma Desktop, with DESIGN.md output overlap.

enhances.context: Union of all six modes' contributions to sk-code implementation: browser debugging for the WEB stack route (mcp-chrome-devtools), task-management context for development workflows (mcp-click-up), agentic-browser task evidence and REPL captures (mcp-aside-devtools), Figma exports/DESIGN.md/variables/screenshots feeding implementation (mcp-figma), and real-app UI reference research grounding what gets built (mcp-refero for web products, mcp-mobbin for mobile app patterns).

siblings.context: Cross-hub sibling: mcp-figma reads design context from Figma Desktop while sk-design's md-generator sources from live URLs, with DESIGN.md output overlap.

key_topics: figma-ds-cli binary (and the unrelated figma-cli npm collision)
Figma Desktop session and local daemon
safe (plugin) vs yolo (app.asar patch) connect modes
optional Framelink figma MCP via Code Mode
derived.causal_summary: mcp-tooling is the single public advisor identity for six MCP tool bridges: three workflow modes and three design transports, folded into one parent hub (packet 126-mcp-tooling-parent, extended to six modes by the aside/refero/mobbin additions). mcp-chrome-devtools routes browser-debugging work between the bdg CLI and Code Mode MCP, prioritizing the cheapest effective interface. mcp-click-up routes ClickUp task management between the cupt CLI (daily ops) and the official ClickUp MCP (documents, goals, bulk operations), with agent-safety invariants (dry-run before batch completion, per-list status resolution). mcp-aside-devtools drives the agentic Aside browser (natural-language tasks and the deterministic repl evidence lane) for autonomous browser work that developer-driven CDP inspection does not cover. mcp-figma drives Figma Desktop from the terminal through figma-ds-cli, gated read-only/mutating/destructive. mcp-refero searches real shipped web-product UI references and mcp-mobbin researches real mobile app/screen/flow patterns, both read-only remote MCP through Code Mode. All three transports carry the mandatory sk-design cross-hub pairing for design judgment before any design-affecting operation -- no transport decides taste itself. It matters because it gives six previously-flat or independently-added MCP bridges one coherent advisor identity while preserving each mode's own contract, and mcp-code-mode -- the shared execution substrate all six reach through Code Mode -- stays external infrastructure rather than a dissolved hub member.
